name: Network Sketcher (Local MCP)
description: Local-first, AI-native MCP server for network design automation. LLM clients such as Cursor and Claude Code create Cisco-style network designs, manage .nsm master files, and generate L1/L2/L3 topology diagrams (SVG / PowerPoint), combined HTML viewers, device tables, and AI Context files from natural-language workflows.
author: cisco-open

localizations.name: Network Sketcher (Local MCP)
localizations.description: ローカルファーストの AI ネイティブ MCP サーバー。Cursor / Claude Code から Cisco スタイルのネットワーク設計、.nsm マスター管理、L1/L2/L3 トポロジ図・統合 HTML ビューア・デバイステーブル・AI Context の生成が可能です。

tools.name: get_workspace_info
tools.description: Return the active workspace path and the list of .nsm master files (and other NS-generated files) in it. Always safe to call.

tools.name: suggest_workspace
tools.description: Suggest OS-appropriate (Windows / macOS / Linux) workspace directory candidates under the user's home, with hints about which exist and are writable. Always safe to call.

tools.name: set_workspace
tools.description: Set the active workspace directory for this session. Validates that the path is under the user's home directory, creates it if missing, and confirms it is writable.

path.description: Absolute path (or '~/...') under the user's home directory.

tools.name: create_empty_master
tools.description: Create a new empty Network Sketcher master file (.nsm) in the active workspace.

filename.description: Optional target name; must start with '[MASTER]' and end with '.nsm'. Defaults to '[MASTER]no_data.nsm'.

tools.name: get_network_state
tools.description: Run the standard set of show commands and return aggregated results - a lightweight summary of the network state for the agent.

master.description: Master filename (e.g. '[MASTER]office.nsm') in the active workspace, or an absolute path under it.

tools.name: get_ai_context
tools.description: Generate the full AI Context file for a master and return its contents (network state plus the full CLI command reference). Recommended bootstrap call before issuing any edits.

master.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

tools.name: run_commands
tools.description: Execute one or more Network Sketcher CLI commands (add / rename / delete / show) against a .nsm master. Multiple lines are batched into a single subprocess. Requires get_ai_context to have been called at least once in this session.

master.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

tools.name: import_master
tools.description: Convert an existing .xlsx master into a .nsm in the active workspace.

xlsx_path.description: Absolute path to the source .xlsx file.

target_name.description: Optional output filename ('[MASTER]*.nsm'). Defaults to the source basename.

tools.name: export_master_xlsx
tools.description: Convert a .nsm master back to .xlsx for use with Excel or the Network Sketcher Offline edition.

master.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

tools.name: export_diagram
tools.description: Export an L1, L2, or L3 network diagram for the given .nsm master (SVG or PPTX).

master.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

area.description: Optional area name for single-area output.

tools.name: export_device_table_html
tools.description: Export an interactive HTML Device Table preview from a .nsm master.

master.description: Master filename in the active workspace (e.g. '[MASTER]office.nsm').

tools.name: build_default_outputs
tools.description: Generate the default deliverable bundle: combined L1/L2/L3 HTML viewer, per-layer SVGs, device table HTML, and AI Context for a .nsm master.

master.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

prompts.name: start_ns_session
prompts.description: Bootstrap a Network Sketcher session: ensure a workspace is active and load AI context for the given master before any edits.

resources.name: Workspace overview
resources.description: JSON snapshot of the active workspace directory and its inventory of .nsm masters and generated files.

resources.name: CLI command reference
resources.description: Full Network Sketcher CLI command reference.
